title: Soil and Plant Scientists
description: Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity.

tasks.text: Communicate research or project results to other professionals or the public or teach related courses, seminars, or workshops.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop methods of conserving or managing soil that can be applied by farmers or forestry companies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide information or recommendations to farmers or other landowners regarding ways in which they can best use land, promote plant growth, or avoid or correct problems such as erosion.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct experiments to develop new or improved varieties of field crops, focusing on characteristics such as yield, quality, disease resistance, nutritional value, or adaptation to specific soils or climates.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate soil problems or poor water quality to determine sources and effects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate responses of soils to specific management practices to determine the use capabilities of soils and the effects of alternative practices on soil productivity.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct experiments to investigate the underlying mechanisms of plant growth and response to the environment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify degraded or contaminated soils and develop plans to improve their chemical, biological, or physical characteristics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop new or improved methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or insect pests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide advice regarding the development of regulatory standards for land reclamation or soil conservation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Study soil characteristics to classify soils on the basis of factors such as geographic location, landscape position, or soil properties.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop improved measurement techniques, soil conservation methods, soil sampling devices, or related technology.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct research to determine best methods of planting, spraying, cultivating, harvesting, storing, processing, or transporting horticultural products.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop environmentally safe methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or pests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Study ways to improve agricultural sustainability, such as the use of new methods of composting.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Consult with engineers or other technical personnel working on construction projects about the effects of soil problems and possible solutions to these problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform chemical analyses of the microorganism content of soils to determine microbial reactions or chemical mineralogical relationships to plant growth.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop ways of altering soils to suit different types of plants.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct experiments investigating how soil forms, changes, or interacts with land-based ecosystems or living organisms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Survey undisturbed or disturbed lands for classification, inventory, mapping, environmental impact assessments, environmental protection planning, conservation planning, or reclamation planning.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan or supervise waste management programs for composting or farming.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Research technical requirements or environmental impacts of urban green spaces, such as green roof installations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct experiments regarding causes of bee diseases or factors affecting yields of nectar or pollen.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify or classify species of insects or allied forms, such as mites or spiders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study insect distribution or habitat and recommend methods to prevent importation or spread of injurious species.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan or supervise land conservation or reclamation programs for industrial development projects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct research into the use of plant species as green fuels or in the production of green fuels.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
